purpose: Define the contract-only SaaS packaging tiers that central governance may recommend while leaving runtime orchestration and delivery execution inside independent services.
control_plane_boundary: Central governance may define tier logic, required inputs, expected receipts, and handoff conditions.
Central governance may not run service-local deployment, domain cutover, or long-lived monitoring agents.
Independent services remain responsible for execution entrypoints, runtime ownership, and environment credentials.

tiers.label: audit-only
handoff_conditions: The customer receives an audit summary and prioritized risk register.
No governance bootstrap or monitoring link is required.

tiers.label: audit-plus-governance-setup
handoff_conditions: The service must expose the governed kernel and structured plan draft.
Governance setup may be delivered, but continuous monitoring is optional.

tiers.label: audit-governance-and-monitoring-link
handoff_conditions: The service must expose governance setup and a live monitoring link contract.
Owner route, evidence freshness cadence, and escalation expectations must be explicit.

anti_patterns: Selling tier-1 as if governance bootstrap has already been installed.
Treating tier-2 as sufficient when the customer explicitly expects ongoing monitoring coverage.
Using central governance to execute runtime orchestration instead of constraining and reviewing it.
